actionable_feedback: Embed explicit cost estimates for each recommended mitigation and show how they sum against decision_budget_usd (205000). Provide a simple table (record | recommended action | estimated_hours | unit_cost_usd | estimated_cost_usd | justification) and a one-line sum comparison to decision_budget_usd with a recommendation (e.g., proceed/phase/decline).

Make the workbook auditable: ensure key computed fields are formula-backed (not screenshots). Add a 'Formulas' worksheet that lists the exact cell formulas used (or place formulas directly in the cells). If environment parsing is unreliable, include the exact Excel formulas as text in incident_review.md appendix.

Include reproducibility artifacts: a CSV of the computed fields (stress_score, predicted_stress, residual_pct, status) and a short script (Python or a small Excel macro / documented Excel formulas) that reproduces the calculations from raw input columns. Attach these files or embed them in the repository/artifacts.

Add a small sensitivity table showing the effect of varying the sensitivity_shift_pct (e.g., +/-5% and +/-10%) on statuses and counts (needs_review_count, confirmed_issue_count). Explicitly call out sample-size limitations (4 records) and avoid causal language; keep attribution cautious.

In incident_review.md, add a one-paragraph checklist confirming that the .xlsx contains auditable formulas and that the CSV/script reproduces the workbook outputs, so verifiers can validate without runtime library access.
artifact_specific_comments: incident_review.md: clear method and per-record table; add explicit cost mapping and formula appendix as noted.
abr_qoe_attribution.xlsx: verifier could not programmatically inspect formulas due to environment limits-ensure formulas are stored in workbook cells and add a 'Formulas' sheet documenting them.
feedback_summary: Deliverables are present and arithmetically consistent: composite stress formula, monotone linear fit (intercept and slope), per-record predicted stresses, residual_pct, and status assignments are documented and match trace computed values. The submission lacks an explicit numeric mapping of recommended mitigations to the provided decision_budget_usd (205000), which the rubric required. A secondary, minor verification issue: programmatic inspection of .xlsx formulas could not be performed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so byte-level presence of formulas was not independently validated.

extra_model_fields.notes: Primary semantic deficiency is missing explicit budget-to-mitigation numeric mapping; verifier limitation is environmental (openpyxl unavailable).
recommended_files_to_update: incident_review.md (appendix: exact formulas + sensitivity table + cost mapping)
abr_qoe_attribution.xlsx (add 'Formulas' worksheet and ensure formulas exist in cells)
repro_scripts/recompute.py (small script to recompute values from raw inputs)

verifier_issues_summary.description: Could not open/inspect .xlsx formulas programmatically (openpyxl unavailable). Numeric consistency was confirmed through incident_review.md and trace values, but byte-level formula presence in the workbook was not independently verified.
